pi_items.source: VitalDB dataset verification using vitaldb Python library v1.6.0. Case 6 successfully loaded and confirmed to exist in the publicly available VitalDB dataset containing 6,388 surgical cases.

VitalDB dataset verification. Successfully loaded case 6 data using vitaldb.load_case(6, 'Solar8000/HR', interval=1). Track confirmed to exist and contain valid heart rate data from GE Solar 8000 monitor.
Task specification confirms '10 minutes' window, which equals 600 seconds. Verified by extracting first 600 time points from loaded case data.

Direct analysis of case 6 Solar8000/HR data using vitaldb Python library. Loaded complete heart rate track for case 6 with 1-second sampling interval, extracted all 600 data points for time window 0-600 seconds, and computed maximum value. Found 299 valid (non-NaN) heart rate measurements within this window. Maximum HR value = 118.0 BPM, occurring at approximately 212-217 seconds into the recording.

Tolerance specification in task.json. This is a parameter that defines acceptable error margin for GUI observation (±2.0 BPM). Verified value is the same as metadata value, indicating this is correctly specified as an acceptance criterion.

privileged_info_summary: VitalDB case 6 with Solar8000/HR track (real surgical patient data, not synthetic) contains a peak heart rate of 118.0 BPM during the first 600 seconds (0-10 minutes) of the intraoperative recording. This occurs at approximately 212-217 seconds into the recording. The metadata incorrectly specifies the ground truth peak as 105.0 BPM, which is 13 BPM lower than the actual verified value. All other metadata (case ID, track name, time window) are correct and verified against the actual VitalDB dataset.
